title: pedicularis gruina subsp. gruina
intro: Pedicularis gruina subsp. gruina is a plant species that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as the broomrape family, and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and act as a diuretic. Its health benefits include treating conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and kidney stones, as well as aiding in the treatment of edema and fever. Pedicularis gruina subsp. gruina can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate various health complaints.
